title: Drymosiphon grandiflorus

intro: Drymosiphon grandiflorus, also known as the "Tahina", is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Madagascar, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, infections, and other inflammatory conditions.
